All rights reserved. @@ -28,0 +28,5 @@ +import java.lang.classfile.ClassFile; +import java.lang.classfile.ClassFileVersion; + +import jdk.internal.javac.PreviewFeature; + @@ -29,9 +34,12 @@ - * Class file format versions of the Java virtual machine. - * - * See the appropriate edition of The Java Virtual Machine - * Specification for information about a particular class file - * format version. - * - *

Note that additional class file format version constants will be - * added to model future releases of the Java Virtual Machine - * Specification. + * A {@code class} file format version represents a set of VM features, derived + * from a supported class {@linkplain ClassFileVersion#majorVersion() major} and + * {@linkplain ClassFileVersion#minorVersion() minor} version combination. + * The appropriate edition of The Java Virtual Machine Specification + * for each Java SE release lists its supported major and minor version + * combinations and their correspoding format versions. + *

+ * Additional class file format version constants will be added to model future + * class file formats defined by future releases of the JVMS. + *

+ * A special constant, {@link #PREVIEW_ENABLED}, represents the VM features of + * the current Java SE release when preview features are enabled. @@ -40,5 +48,4 @@ - * The complete version used in a class file includes a major version - * and a minor version; this enum only models the major version. A - * Java virtual machine implementation is required to support a range - * of major versions; see the corresponding edition of the The - * Java Virtual Machine Specification for details. + * A class file format version is derived from a supported class major and minor + * version combination. Given a supported combination, the major version alone + * is sufficient to identify most class file format versions; see {@link + * #fromMajor(int)} and JVMS {@jvms 4.1}. @@ -54,1 +61,1 @@ - * convenience, but they are not modeled by this enum. + * convenience, but they are not modeled by the RELEASE_ constants. @@ -92,0 +99,1 @@ + * 25: no changes @@ -398,1 +406,0 @@ - ; // Reduce code churn when appending new constants @@ -400,2 +407,25 @@ - // Note to maintainers: when adding constants for newer releases, - // the implementation of latest() must be updated too. + // Note to maintainers: Add new constants right above. + // The implementation of latest() must be updated too. + /** + * All VM features of the {@linkplain #latest() current Java SE release} + * when preview features are enabled. This includes all preview VM features + * of the current Java SE release, which may not be supported by later Java + * SE releases. + * + * @apiNote + * This is a reflective preview API to allow tools running in Java runtime + * environments with no preview feature enabled to examine preview VM + * features, such as those present in {@code class} files. + *

+ * This constant represents different sets of features on runtimes of + * different Java SE releases. Programmers should check the current Java + * SE version to ensure they are accessing the desired set of features. + * + * @since 27 + * @jls 1.5 Preview Features + * @jvms 1.5 Preview Features + * @see + * JEP 12: Preview Features + */ + @PreviewFeature(feature = PreviewFeature.Feature.LANGUAGE_MODEL, reflective = true) + PREVIEW_ENABLED(ClassFile.latestMajorVersion()); @@ -464,1 +494,1 @@ - * + *

@@ -466,1 +496,2 @@ - * RELEASE_6} have non-{@code null} results. + * #RELEASE_6}, except {@link #PREVIEW_ENABLED}, have + * non-{@code null} results. @@ -469,0 +500,2 @@ + if (this == PREVIEW_ENABLED) + return null;
